source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Lifting Incident

event.top_event: Dropped Load - Steel Plate Striking Personnel
actions_taken: Both injured personnel reported to medic
Both personnel evacuated to University Hospital in New Orleans, LA
BSEE Houma District office notified orally and written report submitted within 15 days
Multiple tests conducted with magnetic heavy lifting device
Manufacturer testing conducted on the device
3rd party annual lifting gear inspection report reviewed
event.summary: On December 25, 2024, a 340 lbs steel plate disengaged from a magnetic heavy lifting device on the Noble Drillship Blackhornet while being lowered to the deck. The plate fell 47 inches and struck both a Noble welder (IP1) and a Noble roustabout (IP2) on the top of the left foot. Both personnel sustained swelling and contusions and were evacuated to University Hospital. Investigation determined the magnetic heavy lifting device was defective with damaged pole shoes causing air gaps that reduced lifting capacity. Contributing factors included inadequate job safety analysis, lack of timeout for safety (TOFS), failure to review hazards, and work environment constraints that created time pressure.
recommendations: BSEE Houma District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations issue a safety alert/bulletin regarding this incident
key_phrases: air gaps between pole shoe and part

threats.name: Magnetic lifting device pole shoes damaged causing reduced lifting capacity
Inadequate job safety analysis not identifying lifting device failure hazard
No timeout for safety (TOFS) called before resuming lift operation
Personnel working in proximity to suspended load without hazard review
Blocked walkway creating time pressure to move plate quickly

consequences.name: Swelling and contusions to both injured personnel
Evacuation of injured personnel requiring hospitalization
Potential for catastrophic injury or death from dropped load

controls.name: Annual lifting gear inspection and certification

supporting_text: The BSEE Houma District office was notified orally and a written report was submitted within 15 days
The BSEE investigation found the magnetic heavy lifting device was certified on March 19, 2024, by a 3rd party company, as part of an annual lifting gear inspection
at the time of that inspection, the equipment was documented to be in good working order
the magnetic heavy lifting device was damaged and could not lift to its full capacity

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.lod_basis: Pre-job hazard assessment and work planning

supporting_text: While this work order covered the hazards associated with the hot work being performed, it did not adequately cover the specific hazards associated with the lifting equipment
While the JSA did note Pinch, Caught Between, and Struck by as a hazard, it did not specifically identify the magnetic heavy lifting device prematurely releasing the plate as a hazard
BSEE Investigators found the JSA to be inadequate
The JSA did not have a proper hazard analysis
The JSA did not have a proper management of change

controls.name: Timeout for Safety (TOFS)
controls.barrier_role: Work stoppage and hazard review before task resumption
controls.lod_basis: Safety management system requiring pause and review

supporting_text: The job was not stopped, a timeout for safety (TOFS) was not called, and the potential hazards of the job were not discussed
IP2 made a personal decision to help IP1 without stopping the job, calling a TOFS, or reviewing the JSA that was in place
BSEE Investigators concluded IP1 and IP2 exhibited employee perceived haste by not stopping the job and reviewing the JSA

controls.name: Supervision and Work Authorization
controls.barrier_role: Oversight of work activities and hazard review
controls.lod_basis: Management oversight and stop work authority

supporting_text: IP1 did not take the time to review the JSA with IP2
There was inadequate stop work authority
Supervision – IP1 did not take the time to review the JSA with IP2

controls.name: Work Environment Layout and Housekeeping
controls.barrier_role: Prevent congestion and time pressure in work areas
controls.lod_basis: Facility design and work area management

supporting_text: The walkway was blocked by the plate not allowing IP2 and the other roustabout enough room to pass with the pallets
IP2 and another Noble roustabout did not have enough room to maneuver the pallets around the plate left on the work stands
Work environment – The walkway was blocked by the plate not allowing IP2 and the other roustabout enough room to pass with the pallets

controls.name: Magnetic lifting device mechanical integrity
controls.barrier_role: Prevent premature load release due to equipment defect

supporting_text: Manufacturer tests were conducted and the magnetic heavy lifting device was determined to be defective

Pole shoes are in rough shape, whenever a big ding or hard landing causing dents on pole shoes allows air gaps between the pole shoe and part being lifted, this will cause the tool to not lift to its full capacity
the magnetic heavy lifting device was damaged and could not lift to its full capacity
the pole shoes were damaged likely by impacts or hard landings which caused dents on pole shoes creating air gaps that affected the device to have a decreased lifting capacity

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
